Patch Set 2:

I'm still looking into this, but I'm not yet convinced this actually tests the failure fixed by https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51559/

It looks like calling "lfs setstripe" on a file directly does not go through lod_get_default_lov_striping() and thus doesn't set the relevant memory in the MDT thread's environment (struct lod_default_striping *lds = lod_lds_buf_get(env)). I think to set the memory and trigger failure requires setting specific striping on a directory and then creating a file in that directory.

So I think to test the failure would require something like this instead: 

save_layout_restore_at_exit $MOUNT
$LFS setstripe -o 0,1 $MOUNT
touch $DIR/$tfile.{1..128}

I'm not 100% confident on this yet, so let me know if you disagree and think this will hit the failure. (I did test this several times locally without the fix in place and it never failed for me.)

This is a guess, but I think a possible sequence of events in the failing case may start with these events in test_27H:

$LFS setstripe -o 0 -o 2 $DIR/$tdir || error "setstripe failed"
touch $DIR/$tdir/$tfile

Patch Set 2:

We don't have to se the default layout on $MOUNT for the test to work. The test can set it on $tdir and create non-append files in the directory to test load up the threads.

parents.subject: LU-16872 lod: reset llc_ostlist when using O_APPEND stripes

commit.subject: LU-16872 tests: exercise sanity test_27M more fully
commit.message:
LU-16872 tests: exercise sanity test_27M more fully

Improve the sanity.sh test_27M to precreate a bunch of files with
specific OST striping so that it is more likely to trigger the code
path that accessed the stale OST list when using O_APPEND layout.

Also clean up code style in the rest of this subtest.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity env=ONLY=27M,ONLY_REPEAT=200
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ie94e3a32fc48198e4e15f44a55d1f8ccf61c74f5
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51602
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Thomas Bertschinger <bertschinger@lanl.gov>
Reviewed-by: Alex Deiter <alex.deiter@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
